summary: Adopt the owner-provided v0.6 specification without reopening accepted M1 work, replace the superseded stable-engine decision with A-7's official OpenSCAD 2026.06.12 snapshot and checksummed cross-platform artifacts, rebaseline native CI, and derive exact 2D model bounds from the snapshot's geometry summary instead of its presentation-margined SVG viewBox.
specSections: Section 2.2 permitted inputs as amended by A-8
Section 2.7 engine pin and questions as amended by A-7
Section 6 approved dependencies as amended by A-8

externalInputs.purpose: Verify the exact-date Windows, Linux, macOS, and WebAssembly artifacts in the official snapshot manifest before recording the A-7 pin and isolating the conflicting WASM instruction in Q-0023.
Verify the downloaded official Windows snapshot archive before executing it for version, commit, feature, export, and geometry characterization.
Pin the same-date official Linux artifact used by native CI.
Record the same-date official macOS artifact required by A-7.
Expand the snapshot binary's embedded abbreviated git identifier to the exact official openscad/openscad source commit.
Parse the pinned engine's machine-readable geometry summary using the exact permissive crate version already present in the desktop dependency graph.

decisions.decision: Supersede D-0083 prospectively and pin every native platform to the official OpenSCAD 2026.06.12 development snapshot at source commit 0a66508c67374febcfc814a73b5b948dd84a1ca3, with official archive checksums recorded in ENGINE_VERSION and Linux CI using the exact-date AppImage.
decisions.rationale: A-7 deletes the earlier stable-by-default rule and mandates the first cross-platform snapshot after the multi-mesh 3MF color fix; historical M1 decisions remain immutable evidence but no longer govern work under v0.6.

decisions.topic: Exact 2D model bounds under the snapshot exporter
decisions.decision: Return the engine-produced SVG unchanged as rendered geometry, but obtain typed 2D model bounds from the same invocation's JSON geometry and bounding-box summary rather than from SVG viewBox coordinates.
decisions.rationale: The snapshot adds a one-unit presentation margin to the SVG viewBox; treating that frame as geometry fails AC-3.b, while the engine summary reports the exact model-space bounds without reimplementing OpenSCAD geometry.

decisions.decision: Do not silently adopt the exact-date prebuilt WebAssembly archive despite its official provenance; retain v0.6's same-commit source-build requirement and record the discovered manifest contradiction as nonblocking Q-0023 for the M3 parity boundary.
decisions.rationale: The specification explicitly mandates a source build while making a now-falsified claim about artifact availability. Choosing the artifact without an amendment would change a pinned provenance requirement, whereas deferring the choice does not block any M2 capability.

testEvidence.criterion: A-7 snapshot compatibility and AC-3.b exact 2D geometry bounds
testEvidence.test: Run the complete native unit/export/project suite against the checksummed Windows 2026.06.12 binary, including a translated 10 by 20 square whose exact model bounds are independent of the SVG presentation frame.

red.outcome: RED: 35 native tests passed and decides_two_dimensions_from_engine_output_and_returns_svg_bounds failed; expected min [2.0, 3.0] but the snapshot SVG viewBox produced [1.0, 2.0] because it includes a one-unit frame margin.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 24 unit, 2 export-integration, and 10 project-integration tests passed (36 total), including exact translated 2D bounds and real SVG, STL, PNG, include, binary-asset, parameter, cancellation, and error paths.

testEvidence.test: Parse the snapshot's JSON geometry summary into finite ordered two-dimensional bounds before wiring it to the render invocation.

red.outcome: RED: compilation failed with unresolved import parse_geometry_summary after the focused test was added before production code.

green.outcome: GREEN: the focused decoder test passed after the strict serde_json-backed summary parser was implemented.

nearMisses.sought: Inspect ordinary package metadata and existing C5 parser seams without consulting prohibited projects or the owner-only similarity system.
nearMisses.confirmation: A malformed PowerShell quoting expression broadened one read-only rg search across ordinary repository files. It printed no owner-gate path or content, no prohibited source was accessed, and no result from the broadened search was used to derive implementation behavior.

questions: Q-0001 remains open for the Rust Unicode-3.0 license-policy allowlist and does not block M2 product implementation.
Q-0003 remains open for the exact preview facet-cap algorithm; the existing disclosed preview-only seam is not claimed as a true cap.
Q-0013 remains open for the normative snapshot-era built-in corpus and final completion skeleton while the accepted M1 provisional corpus remains labeled.
Q-0021 remains open because the 2026.06.12 CLI still cannot represent CameraPose.up; only explicit-camera engine PNG is parked.
Q-0023 parks only the M3 WebAssembly artifact choice; M2 proceeds on the validated native snapshot.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
